summary: Not ready for PR acceptance. The deterministic evidence for 8a2c197 is materially improved: repo validation, pytest, and the range-bound diff check have durable outputs and the previous whitespace blocker is fixed for main..8a2c197. Acceptance is still blocked because the live provider-mirrored Claude review evidence failed to produce the required normalized reviewer reports, and the source still has an uncovered nonzero-provider-exit path that writes schema-invalid failed invocation metadata.

findings.title: Live Claude provider-mirrored reviews failed without normalized reviewer reports
evidence: docs/pr-merge-readiness.md:32-44 lists missing live Claude evidence, failed external reviewer invocation metadata when live evidence is required, and mismatched/missing normalized report evidence as blocking surfaces.

run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/review-prompt-contract.yaml:136-175 declares required claude-code assignments and report paths for verification-claude, architecture-claude, and adversarial-claude.

run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/reviewer-invocation.verification-claude.json:40-47 records exit_code 0 but failure_stage provider_output_processing and points normalized_output_path at reviewer-report.verification-claude.json.
run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/reviewer-invocation.architecture-claude.json:40-47 records the same provider_output_processing failure for architecture-claude.
run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/reviewer-invocation.adversarial-claude.json:40-47 records the same provider_output_processing failure for adversarial-claude.

A read-only file listing of the rerun artifact root showed no reviewer-report.verification-claude.json, reviewer-report.architecture-claude.json, or reviewer-report.adversarial-claude.json at the time of review.
findings.blocker_path: pr-merge-readiness requires provider-mirrored live Claude review evidence for the declared reviewer assignments -> the current live Claude invocations failed during provider output processing and did not produce normalized reviewer-report JSON files -> the readiness evaluator must treat the external review evidence as failed/missing.
findings.acceptance_impact: The branch cannot be accepted as PR-ready because the required provider-mirrored review gate is incomplete and the previous live-Claude-normalization blocker has recurred for material_change_id 8a2c197.
findings.recommendation: Fix the live reviewer prompt/output path or rerun/normalize the Claude reports through an accepted path, then refresh the review invocation evidence and fusion/finding-validation artifacts for the same material_change_id.
findings.calibration_reason: P1 because this is mandatory live review evidence for the selected pr-merge-readiness topology; accepting without it would violate the workflow's stated blocking policy.

findings.title: Nonzero provider exits still write invocation metadata that cannot satisfy the updated schema
evidence: run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/evidence-report.md:41-42 claims failed external reviewer invocation metadata is now schema-valid evidence and is not counted as completed external review evidence.
schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json:217-227 requires normalized_output_hash unless failure_stage is present; when failure_stage is present it requires failure_message.
scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py:937-953 writes invocation metadata for exit_code != 0 without adding failure_stage, failure_message, or normalized_output_hash, then raises.

scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py:1003-1014 only adds failure_stage/failure_message when the invocation file does not already exist, so the nonzero-exit file written at lines 941-951 is not repaired by the generic exception handler.
tests/test_scripts_smoke.py:5013-5050 covers schema-valid provider_output_processing failures for zero-exit non-JSON output, but I found no corresponding regression for a real provider nonzero exit.
findings.blocker_path: 8a2c197 claims to fix failed external-review invocation evidence -> one real provider failure path still emits metadata missing both the success hash and failure discriminator required by the schema -> failed external review evidence can remain schema-invalid.
findings.acceptance_impact: Accepting the branch would leave the prior failed-external-review-evidence blocker only partially fixed; a live Claude process failure can still produce invocation evidence that cannot validate under the branch's own schema.
findings.recommendation: Set failure_stage and failure_message before writing invocation metadata in the exit_code != 0 branch, or route that branch through the same failure writer, and add a regression that simulates claude_code.invoke returning a nonzero exit and validates the produced invocation JSON against schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json.
findings.calibration_reason: P1 because the code contradicts the rerun's fix evidence for a mandatory external-review failure-evidence path and can invalidate gate evidence on real provider failures.

findings.title: Referenced local pr-merge-readiness binding still carries prior run metadata
evidence: run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/verification-gate-report.md:10-11 cites target content head 8a2c197 and binding .agentsflow/workflows/pr-merge-readiness.binding.yaml.
.agentsflow/workflows/pr-merge-readiness.binding.yaml:10 records run_id 2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness, not the rerun id.
.agentsflow/workflows/pr-merge-readiness.binding.yaml:49 records raw_root under the previous run id.
.agentsflow/workflows/pr-merge-readiness.binding.yaml:124 records target_content_head fc5f09c, the prior blocked material change.
findings.why_it_matters: This did not block my review because the assigned packet, run.yaml, command evidence, and prompt contract all carry 8a2c197, and .agentsflow is an ignored project overlay. It still weakens replayability of the gate report because the cited binding is stale for the rerun.
findings.recommendation: Refresh or remove run-specific fields from the local binding before using it as a cited gate input for the rerun.
findings.no_blocker_path_reason: I did not find evidence that the stale binding fields were consumed by the current readiness evaluator or by the assigned review packet; the concrete P1 blockers are the missing live Claude reports and the wrapper failure path.

requests_for_additional_verification.request: After fixing the Claude output/normalization issue, rerun the live provider-mirrored Claude reviewers and verify that all declared reviewer-report.*-claude.json files exist, are schema-valid, and match their invocation hashes.
Add a targeted regression for claude_code.invoke returning exit_code != 0 and validate the generated reviewer-invocation JSON against schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json.

self_declared_limitations: Read-only review only: I did not modify files, create patches, update evidence, run tests, or execute repository scripts.
I used static file inspection, hash checks, and read-only git range inspection. I did not exhaustively audit all 337 changed files outside verification evidence and external-review failure handling.
